scope.task_text: Checkpoint absorbed workflow observation boundary attack layers after three self-red-team rounds

artifact_boundary.statement: This checkpoint summarizes exercised attack layers and current scope limits. It is not a claim that attack-space coverage is sufficient.

current_state.summary: repeatable boundary absorption pattern established; attack-space coverage still unknown

taxonomy_orientation.warning: layer coverage must not be mistaken for misuse-intent coverage

attacks: diagnostic metadata appeared on a policy-shaped path
boundary_gain: state precedence now preserves trust-linkage failure instead of flattening it into freshness-only output
diagnostic metadata is separated from policy-facing state semantics

attacks: observation_coverage drifted toward score, ranking, or threshold meaning
boundary_gain: helper surfaces now separate policy semantics from diagnostic-only fields
metric semantics now explicitly forbid score, ranking, threshold, and compliance-proxy use

attacks: legal observation surfaces were recomposed into pseudo-policy, severity, attention-priority, or escalation views
boundary_gain: cross-surface recomposition is explicitly forbidden in prose and machine-readable contract
surface roles are explicit enough to make recomposition misuse reviewable

common_failure_pattern.name: structure_affordance_drift
common_failure_pattern.statement: Across the first three attack layers, structure, helper affordance, and composition paths were more likely than prose alone to shape downstream interpretation.
common_failure_pattern.review_priority_rule: future boundary review should prioritize structure, helper affordances, and composition paths before naming or prose cleanup

misuse_intent_overlay.question: Can observation outputs be read as implying that attention or escalation is now justified?
Can observation outputs be read as a severity taxonomy or seriousness label?
Can observation outputs be used to sort, prioritize, or queue work or reviews?
Can observation outputs be read as showing workflow compliance or non-compliance?

failure_threshold.rule: If any attack can derive attention, severity, escalation, or other pseudo-policy conclusions without violating explicit contract text, the boundary is not progression-ready.
failure_threshold.reason: Repair activity must not be mistaken for progression readiness while legal pseudo-policy paths still exist.
trigger_examples: attention_priority can be reconstructed from legal observation surfaces
severity_label can be derived without breaking an explicit contract rule
action_escalation can be inferred from legal state or metric interpretation

not_yet_explicitly_pressured: presentation-induced reinterpretation on a future consumer surface
external reviewer attack quality beyond self-red-team
behavior of a first constrained consumer attachment
